s101.title: Powers of Inspection
s101.auditorFocus: JPDP inspectors will assess whether the organisation is prepared to cooperate with inspections and has a documented inspection response procedure. The state of the compliance document repository is a direct reflection of the organisation's overall compliance maturity.

evidenceItems.name: Compliance document repository (completeness and currency)
evidenceItems.description: A central repository containing all key PDPA compliance documents, organised and readily accessible to produce to JPDP inspectors promptly
whatGoodLooksLike: Repository contains all key documents: ROPA, registration certificate, DPO appointment, PDPA policy, DPAs, retention schedule, disposal logs, privacy notices, training records, breach log, access request log, and security policy
All documents are current (last-reviewed within the past 12 months, or within the applicable review cycle)
Documents are organised by category and retrievable within minutes
Repository is maintained in both physical (for key signed originals) and digital formats
Repository completeness is reviewed against a checklist quarterly by the DPO
commonGaps: Key compliance documents are scattered across multiple systems, email inboxes, and network drives with no central access
Repository exists but key documents are out of date (e.g., retention schedule from 2020, expired DPAs)
Repository completeness has never been formally reviewed against a checklist
Physical documents (signed originals of DPAs, registration certificate) cannot be located
suggestedSources: Compliance document repository (digital and/or physical)
Repository completeness checklist and last-review date
DPO quarterly repository review records
evidenceItems.retentionPeriod: Per the retention schedule applicable to each document type

evidenceItems.name: Inspection response procedure and preparedness self-assessment
evidenceItems.description: A documented procedure for responding to JPDP inspections, and evidence of annual self-assessments conducted to identify and remediate compliance gaps
whatGoodLooksLike: Inspection response procedure designates the DPO as primary contact and specifies notification of Legal counsel upon receiving an inspection notice
Inspection response procedure assigns responsibilities for document collection, witness preparation, and escort of inspection officers
Annual self-assessment is conducted using the JPDP inspection checklist as a guide
Self-assessment findings are documented with remediation actions and completion dates
Self-assessment report is presented to management to resource gap remediation
commonGaps: No inspection response procedure exists
No self-assessments have ever been conducted
Self-assessment was conducted but findings were not acted upon — the same gaps are present at the actual inspection
suggestedSources: Inspection response procedure document
Annual PDPA self-assessment report (last 2 years)
Remediation tracker from self-assessment
Management acknowledgement of self-assessment findings

evidenceItems.name: System audit logs for personal data processing activities
evidenceItems.description: Audit logs from systems processing personal data, recording access events, data modifications, and export activities with sufficient detail to reconstruct processing activities for inspection purposes
whatGoodLooksLike: Audit logs capture user ID, timestamp, action performed, data category accessed, and source IP for all access to personal data
Logs are tamper-evident (write-once or cryptographically protected) so they cannot be altered retrospectively
Log retention meets the minimum requirement (3 years) so historical access patterns can be reviewed
Logs are searchable and can produce a report of all access by a specific user or to a specific data category within minutes
Regular log review is conducted as part of the security monitoring process, with anomalies investigated
commonGaps: Audit logs are not enabled on the primary systems containing personal data
Logs are enabled but retention is set to 30 or 90 days, making historical review impossible
Logs capture only login events and do not capture data access or export events
Logs are not reviewed — they are collected but never analysed, so security incidents go undetected
Log integrity is not protected — logs could be altered without detection
suggestedSources: Audit log configuration screenshots for each primary system containing personal data
Sample audit log export (demonstrating log content and searchability)
Log retention configuration documentation
Security monitoring review records showing log analysis is being performed

auditTips: At the start of the inspection, ask the organisation to produce its compliance document index — assess how quickly documents are retrieved
Request the most recent self-assessment report and compare the findings with current observations — are the same gaps still present?
Ask who the DPO is and verify they are available to assist during the inspection
Request a sample audit log export from the primary customer data system and verify it contains sufficient detail

s104.title: Complaint Procedures
s104.auditorFocus: JPDP inspectors may have specific data subject complaints they are investigating. They will look for evidence that the organisation has a functioning internal grievance mechanism and that complaints are investigated, remediated, and reported.

evidenceItems.name: Internal data subject complaint procedure (published)
evidenceItems.description: A documented and published internal complaint procedure enabling data subjects to raise PDPA concerns directly with the organisation before escalating to the Commissioner
whatGoodLooksLike: Complaint procedure is published in BM and English, accessible from the privacy notice or organisation's website
Procedure specifies a named contact or team for receiving complaints and a committed response timeframe
Procedure explains what the organisation will do with the complaint (investigate, respond, remediate) and the data subject's right to escalate to the Commissioner
Procedure is tested periodically to verify the contact details are working
Procedure is cross-referenced in the privacy notice so data subjects can easily find it
commonGaps: No internal complaint procedure published — data subjects are directed immediately to the Commissioner without internal resolution opportunity
Complaint contact email is incorrect, unmonitered, or inactive
Procedure does not specify any timeframe for response
Procedure exists but is not linked from the privacy notice, making it inaccessible
suggestedSources: Published complaint procedure (website screenshot or PDF)
Privacy notice showing link to complaint procedure
Test email to complaint contact address confirming it is active

evidenceItems.name: Data subject complaint log and resolution records
evidenceItems.description: A log of all data subject complaints received (direct and via the Commissioner), showing investigation steps, outcomes, and remedial actions taken
whatGoodLooksLike: Complaint log records all complaints with date received, nature, investigation steps, outcome, and remedial actions
Complaints are investigated promptly with a target resolution timeframe
Where a complaint reveals a genuine violation, remediation is documented and the data subject is informed of the outcome
Complaint themes are analysed to identify systemic issues requiring structural remediation
DPO reviews complaint log monthly and reports complaint metrics to management
commonGaps: No complaint log exists
Log records complaints received but does not document investigation steps or outcomes
Where the complaint reveals a genuine violation, no remediation is documented
Complaint log shows a pattern of similar complaints indicating a systemic issue that has not been addressed
suggestedSources: Complaint log (last 3 years)
Sample complaint investigation records and response letters
Remediation actions taken in response to complaints
Management report on complaint metrics

evidenceItems.name: Commissioner investigation correspondence and response records
evidenceItems.description: Records of any Commissioner investigations arising from data subject complaints, including all correspondence with the Commissioner, internal investigation records, and evidence of any remedial actions taken in response
whatGoodLooksLike: All Commissioner correspondence is filed in chronological order and retrievable immediately
The organisation's responses to Commissioner investigation requests are factually accurate, complete, and legally reviewed before submission
Where the Commissioner investigation reveals a violation, the organisation has implemented prompt remedial action documented with evidence
Legal counsel engagement records show Legal was involved from the point the investigation commenced
Post-investigation report documents lessons learned and systemic improvements made
commonGaps: Commissioner correspondence cannot be located or is incomplete
Responses to the Commissioner were prepared without legal review and contain inaccuracies
Remedial action promised in the Commissioner response was never implemented
The organisation failed to respond to the Commissioner within the specified timeframe, resulting in an escalation
suggestedSources: File of all Commissioner correspondence (last 5 years)
Legal counsel engagement records for Commissioner investigations
Internal investigation records and reports
Remediation evidence for violations identified in Commissioner investigations

auditTips: Navigate to the organisation's website and attempt to find the complaint procedure — is it easily accessible?
Review the complaint log for patterns — recurring complaint types indicate systemic compliance issues
Ask whether the organisation has received any complaints that were also lodged with the Commissioner and verify full cooperation
Ask to see the response to any specific complaint cited in the inspection notice or referral

s106.title: Investigation Powers
s106.auditorFocus: JPDP inspectors exercising investigation powers will assess whether the organisation has cooperated fully, provided accurate information, and preserved evidence. An investigation response protocol and evidence preservation capability are the key readiness indicators.

evidenceItems.name: Investigation response protocol and legal counsel engagement records
evidenceItems.description: A documented protocol for managing Commissioner investigations, and evidence that legal counsel was engaged at the earliest opportunity in any past investigation
whatGoodLooksLike: Investigation response protocol designates the CEO and Legal Counsel as ultimate decision-makers and the DPO as operational coordinator
Protocol specifies the internal escalation path from first contact with JPDP investigators to Board notification
Where a past investigation has occurred: legal counsel engagement began within 24 hours of first JPDP contact
Protocol includes guidance on responding to document production requests and interview requests
Protocol specifies the litigation hold activation procedure to prevent evidence destruction
commonGaps: No investigation response protocol exists
Protocol exists but was never tested and staff are unaware of it
In a past investigation, the organisation responded without legal counsel involvement, resulting in inaccurate or prejudicial statements
Litigation hold was not activated promptly, resulting in deletion of potentially relevant records during the investigation
suggestedSources: Investigation response protocol document
Legal counsel engagement records for any past Commissioner investigation
Internal escalation communications from past investigations
Litigation hold activation records

auditTips: Ask for the investigation response protocol and verify it has been reviewed since the 2024 Amendment came into force
If the organisation has been subject to a past Commissioner investigation, verify that all aspects of the investigation are documented and closed
Ask how the organisation would respond to a surprise visit from JPDP investigators — the procedural readiness of the response indicates overall compliance culture
